data.title: Mass Liquidations on October 10, 2025 Draw Trader Attention
data.description: Santiment posted on X that the widespread liquidations reported on October 10, 2025, have captured the attention of traders. The trading community is actively investigating the implications of these events, seeking to understand their impact on the market. The focus remains on analyzing the causes and potential outcomes of these liquidations, as traders aim to navigate the evolving financial landscape.

data.title: Capgemini CEO Highlights AI Adoption as Key to Sales Growth
data.description: Capgemini CEO Aiman Ezzat announced that the French IT company is strategically shifting its focus to support the adoption of artificial intelligence, which is expected to drive sales growth this year. Bloomberg posted on X, emphasizing the company's commitment to integrating AI technologies into its offerings. Ezzat noted that this pivot is a significant move for Capgemini, aligning with industry trends and client demands for advanced technological solutions. The CEO expressed confidence that embracing AI will enhance the company's competitive edge and contribute to its financial performance. As businesses increasingly seek AI-driven innovations, Capgemini aims to position itself as a leader in this transformative sector.

data.title: A whale deposited $4.8 million into HyperLiquid and used 20x leverage to short ETH.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a large whale (0x982...ED8A) deposited $4.8 million USDC into HyperLiquid and opened a 20x leveraged short position in ETH. The ETH short position is currently increasing. Six days ago, this same whale deposited $2.4 million USDC and opened a 10x leveraged short position in HYPE and a 20x leveraged long position in BTC. Currently, its HYPE short position is $8.8 million, its ETH short position is $890,000, and its BTC long position is $3 million.

data.title: Bybit and Mercuryo Offer Zero-Fee Crypto Purchases in Select Markets
data.description:
Bybit partners with Mercuryo to offer zero-fee USDT and USDC purchases via One-Click Buy for select markets from Feb. 4–18, 2026.
source: https://catenaa.com/markets/cryptocurrencies/bybit-and-mercuryo-offer-zero-fee-crypto-purchases-in-select-markets/

data.title: Agentic Economy Gains Momentum Despite Cryptocurrency Price Decline
data.description:
Crypto KOL Haotian Cryptoinsight posted on X that despite the decline in the price of $Molt, the registration of agents on the Moltbook platform has surpassed 2.6 million, indicating a significant platform effect. This development suggests a decoupling of the Agentic Economy from the AI MEME token concept. Over the past two years, the AI Agent token boom and the x402 protocol have been driven by MEME token issuance and wealth creation effects. However, the narrative driven by token prices often leads to its destruction once the wealth myth collapses.

Moltbook is demonstrating a different kind of resilience. Users continue to deploy agents on the platform, which serves as a foundational space for social interaction, identity verification, and command distribution, becoming an incubator for the Agentic Economy. These millions of agents are not only engaging in conversations but are also discovering tasks, executing them, and participating in decentralized governance. They can earn rewards through micropayments facilitated by the x402 protocol.

A commercial ecosystem characterized by value creation, collaboration, and reward distribution is gradually taking shape. Importantly, this growth momentum is not controlled by token prices. The focus has shifted from token prices to the expanding Agentic silicon-based commercial society. Even without the stimulus of token prices, these 2.6 million agents continue to post, interact, trade, and even form 'digital religions' and 'prompt black markets.'

Previously, agents were created for token issuance, but now economic activity is generated through agent interconnectivity. This shift away from the MEME token speculation model towards growth driven by intrinsic platform value marks the true beginning of the Agentic Economy's potential. The outlook for the Agentic Economy remains optimistic.

data.title: XRP Ledger Expands Escrow Functionality with XLS-85 Amendment
data.description: The XRP Ledger has activated the XLS-85 amendment, which extends native escrow functionality to Trustline-based and Multi-Purpose Tokens. According to NS3.AI, this development facilitates secure conditional settlements beyond XRP itself, enhancing the utility of the XRPL for institutional use cases and tokenized finance. The upgrade is expected to potentially increase demand for XRP through higher network activity and fees. While the immediate price effects on XRP remain uncertain, the broader adoption of escrow-enabled tokens could strengthen long-term fundamentals and market sentiment.

data.title: Norway's Wealth Fund Severs Ties with Turkish Finance Firm
data.description: Norway's $2.1 trillion sovereign wealth fund has ended its association with a Turkish financial company following a regulatory fine imposed on one of its senior managers for improper trading activities. Bloomberg posted on X that the decision to cut ties was influenced by the manager's involvement in trading practices that violated established norms. The wealth fund, known for its rigorous ethical standards, regularly reviews its investments to ensure compliance with its guidelines. This move underscores the fund's commitment to maintaining integrity in its investment portfolio. The Turkish firm, which has not been named, is reportedly reassessing its internal controls in response to the incident. The wealth fund's decision highlights the importance of ethical conduct in financial markets and the potential consequences of regulatory breaches.

data.title: Boerse Stuttgart Group to Merge Crypto Operations with Tradias
data.description: German stock exchange operator Boerse Stuttgart Group is planning to merge its cryptocurrency operations with Frankfurt-based digital asset trader Tradias. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this strategic move aims to enhance the capabilities and reach of both entities in the digital asset market. The merger is expected to create a more robust platform for trading and managing digital assets, leveraging the strengths of both companies. This development comes as part of a broader trend of consolidation in the cryptocurrency sector, as firms seek to expand their offerings and improve their competitive edge in the rapidly evolving market.

data.title: U.S. Senate Fails to Advance Homeland Security Funding Bill
data.description: The U.S. Senate was unable to advance a funding bill for the Department of Homeland Security on Thursday, raising concerns about a potential shutdown. According to RTHK, the temporary funding at current levels is set to expire on the 13th of this month, leaving the department at risk of closure. The Senate vote resulted in 52 in favor and 47 against, falling short of the 60 votes needed to move the bill forward. Senate Democrats expressed dissatisfaction with the latest negotiation terms offered by the White House. Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer stated that Democrats are clear in their stance and do not support maintaining the status quo regarding current immigration enforcement. With both the Senate and House of Representatives set to recess for several days, U.S. media widely believe that a shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security is almost inevitable. However, Senate Republican Leader John Thune mentioned that if an agreement on the funding bill is reached, senators would be required to return to the Capitol within 24 hours.

data.title: XRP Declines to $1.30 Amid Bearish Market Trends
data.description: XRP has experienced a decline to approximately $1.30, influenced by bearish market trends and Bitcoin's ongoing price struggles. According to NS3.AI, XRP has dropped nearly 38% this month. Analysts caution that if XRP falls below the $1 support level, it could further decrease to between $0.75 and $0.85, reaching a yearly low. The market conditions remain unfavorable, with the broader impact of Bitcoin potentially dropping to $52,200 and Ethereum falling below $2,000, which could increase downside risks.

data.title: Nestlé Increases Infant Formula Production to Prevent Shortage
data.description: Nestlé is intensifying its production of infant formula in response to a potential shortage. Bloomberg posted on X that this move comes after a contamination issue prompted the Swiss food company and its competitors to recall numerous batches. The recall has raised concerns about supply disruptions, prompting Nestlé to take proactive measures to ensure the availability of infant formula in the market. The company is working to stabilize the supply chain and meet consumer demand amid the ongoing challenges.

data.title: World Liberty Forum to Address Finance and Crypto Intersection Amid Market Challenges
data.description:
The World Liberty Forum, organized by Donald Trump Jr. at Mar-a-Lago, is set to convene on February 18, bringing together 300 leaders from the finance, sports, and cryptocurrency sectors. According to NS3.AI, the event will feature prominent speakers such as Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon, Nasdaq’s Adena Friedman, and CFTC Chairman Michael Selig. These discussions will focus on the convergence of institutional finance and cryptocurrency, particularly in light of stalled crypto legislation.

The forum takes place amid political pressures on U.S. President Donald Trump and a mixed performance in the cryptocurrency market. The market has experienced early gains driven by policy changes, followed by significant losses amounting to $2 trillion. The event aims to address these challenges and explore the future of finance and cryptocurrency integration.

data.title: Matrixport: The market has entered a bear market confirmation phase. Investors should now focus on "when will the next window of opportunity for better asset allocation arrive?"
data.description: Matrixport published an article on the X platform stating: "The recent drop in Bitcoin's price below the key level we highlighted in our October 31, 2025 report has confirmed the downtrend. The current pullback's magnitude and pace are quite similar to historical downtrend phases. For investors, the focus has shifted from 'whether the trend has reversed' to 'when the next more worthwhile investment window will arrive.' Around the period from the end of 2024 to October 2025, Bitcoin repeatedly exhibited characteristics of the near end of its fifth bull market, indicating a gradual weakening of bullish momentum. With the price breaking below the aforementioned key level, this assessment has been confirmed, and the market has entered a bear market confirmation phase."

data.title: Trump Administration's Free Speech Efforts in Europe May Face Challenges
data.description: The Trump administration's efforts to promote its interpretation of free speech in Europe could encounter resistance. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that these attempts might not align with European perspectives on free speech. The administration's approach has sparked debate over the potential implications for international relations and the balance between free expression and regulation. Observers suggest that the differing views on free speech between the U.S. and Europe could lead to diplomatic tensions. As the administration continues to advocate for its stance, the outcome remains uncertain, with potential repercussions for transatlantic relations.

data.title: India's January Palm Oil Imports Surge, Soybean Oil Imports Decline
data.description: India's trade body has reported a significant increase in palm oil imports for January, reaching 766,384 tons, compared to 507,204 tons in December. According to Jin10, this marks a notable rise in palm oil imports. Conversely, soybean oil imports saw a decline, with January figures at 278,888 tons, down from 505,112 tons in December. This shift in import patterns reflects changing dynamics in India's edible oil market.

data.title: DASH Short Position Maintains High Leverage
data.description: On February 13, a significant short position in DASH continues to hold, according to BlockBeats. HyperInsight monitoring reveals that the position is maintained with a 5x leverage, involving 138,776.38 DASH. The average entry price for this position is $71.41 per DASH, resulting in a floating profit of $5.172 million per coin. The address currently holds no other token positions.

data.title: Solana's Project 0 to Conduct Internal Audit After Service Disruption
data.description: Solana ecosystem's prime brokerage, Project 0, is set to conduct an internal audit and review following a service disruption caused by an upstream service interruption. According to Foresight News, this initiative aims to minimize the impact of similar incidents on user experience in the future. The recent disruption, attributed to a downtime at upstream service provider Supabase, did not affect any on-chain positions or user funds.

data.title: Bitcoin Futures Open Interest Declines Amid Bearish Sentiment
data.description: Total Bitcoin futures open interest has decreased by 28% to $34 billion, reaching its lowest level since November 2024. According to NS3.AI, while the open interest measured in Bitcoin remains stable, the decline is attributed to $5.2 billion in forced liquidations over the past two weeks. This trend reflects a bearish market sentiment, influenced by weak U.S. employment data and Bitcoin options skew. Despite these challenges, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs continue to experience strong trading volumes, suggesting ongoing institutional interest.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 600 USDT with a 2.63% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 13, 2026, 05:58 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 600 USDT and is now trading at 599.289978 USDT, with a narrowed 2.63% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Yingda Real Estate Sells Hokkaido Hotel for $80 Million
data.description: Yingda Real Estate has announced the sale of its hotel, Midtown Niseko, located in Hokkaido, Japan, for $80 million. According to Ming Pao, the buyer of the property is Oaktree. This transaction marks a significant move for Yingda Real Estate as it divests from its Japanese holdings. The sale of Midtown Niseko is part of the company's broader strategy to optimize its asset portfolio. Details regarding the future plans for the hotel under its new ownership have not been disclosed.
